question: Implement a `StringWrapper` class that manages a C-style string (`char*`) internally. Provide a parameterized constructor, a copy constructor, a copy assignment operator, and a destructor to correctly handle dynamic memory allocation and deallocation for the string data.

test_cases.expected:
s1: "Hello" (length: 5)
s2: "Hello" (length: 5)
s3: "" (length: 0)
s3 after assignment: "Hello" (length: 5)
s4: "World" (length: 5)
s3 after re-assignment: "World" (length: 5)
s6 after longer assignment: "Longer string test" (length: 18)
s8 after very long assignment: "Very long string that needs reallocation" (length: 40)

errors: The copy assignment operator `operator=` has a memory leak. It allocates new memory for `_data` without first deallocating the existing memory that `_data` pointed to, causing the original memory block to become unreachable and unfreeable. This occurs every time an assignment is made to an already initialized `StringWrapper` object.
